commonProblems.h2: Nissan Platina Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Platina's Renault-sourced K4M 1.6L engine is generally robust but suffers from well-documented fuel system issues. Injector failure and running-rich symptoms dominate replacement enquiries for this grey-import model. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Fuel Injector Failure - Why Nissan Platina K4M Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Platina 1.6i Base, Comfort, Luxury (2002-2010, K4M-706/714/744)
problems.rootCause: Defective injection valves on cylinder 3 cause uneven fuel delivery, leading to rich running, power loss and eventual injector seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean and flow-test injectors; replace seals if viable
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; only suitable before catastrophic injector failure occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty cylinder 3 injector with OEM-spec unit; recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if other injectors test within spec; documented repair cost £515

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K4M engine with upgraded injectors, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage Platina or multiple injector failures

problems.recommendation: On a Nissan Platina valued at £1,500-£3,500, a single injector replacement at £420-£680 makes sense if the engine is otherwise healthy. For vehicles over 100,000 km with multiple symptoms, a rebuilt K4M unit offers better long-term value and eliminates recurrence risk.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K4M engine prices for your Nissan Platina

problems.h4: Engine Running Rich - Power Loss & Increased Fuel Consumption
problems.rootCause: Faulty oxygen sensors combined with worn injectors cause the ECU to over-fuel, resulting in black smoke, rough idle and 20-30% higher fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace upstream lambda sensor; clear fault codes and road test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if injectors still within tolerance; quick diagnostic first step

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors with matched set; ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when running rich persists after sensor replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, sensors and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage examples or repeated rich-running failures

problems.recommendation: Start with an oxygen sensor diagnostic (£180-£290). If rich running continues, budget for a full injector set. For Platina owners facing repeated repairs, a rebuilt K4M engine at £850-£1,700 eliminates the cycle and restores reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Platina running rich repair or replacement

problems.rootCause: Alternator wear combined with aged batteries causes undervoltage, triggering ECU faults, dim lights and intermittent no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new AGM battery; test charging system voltage under load
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if alternator output remains above 13.8V; documented cost £290

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace alternator with refurbished OEM-spec unit; belt tension check
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when battery voltage drops below 12.4V at idle

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new ancillaries; full electrical diagnostic
repairOptions.longevity: Consider only if undervoltage has caused ECU or sensor damage

problems.recommendation: For a 2005 Platina with 215,000 km, start with a £150-£240 battery replacement. If charging issues persist, budget £310-£490 for an alternator. Only pursue engine replacement if diagnostic confirms ECU damage from prolonged undervoltage.
problems.cta: Find Nissan Platina electrical diagnostic and engine specialists

problems.group: Rough Idle & Stalling at Low Speeds
problems.h4: Rough Idle & Stalling at Low Speeds
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Platina 1.6i 16V all trims (2002-2010, K4M series)
problems.rootCause: Clogged air filters combined with injector wear disrupt the air-fuel ratio at idle, causing hesitation, stalling at traffic lights and cold-start difficulties.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Air Filter & Throttle Body Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace air filter; clean throttle body and MAF sensor; reset idle adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Effective first step; resolves stalling if injectors are still functional

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Ultrasonic injector cleaning; test oxygen and cam/crank sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when stalling persists after basic service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, sensors and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for high-mileage Platina with recurring idle issues

problems.recommendation: Begin with a £75-£140 air filter and throttle service. If stalling continues, invest in injector cleaning (£240-£390). For Platina owners experiencing repeated failures, a rebuilt K4M engine provides a permanent solution with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare Nissan Platina engine replacement prices for rough idle fixes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Platina
finalCta.paragraph: The Nissan Platina's K4M engine issues are well-understood by UK specialists familiar with grey-import Renault-platform vehicles. Whether you need a single injector replacement or a full rebuilt engine, EngineMarket connects you with vetted suppliers who understand the Platina's unique requirements. Every rebuilt unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - all delivered nationwide without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Platina Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Platina and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Nissan Platina Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When

years.description: The Nissan Platina launched in Mexico with the Renault K4M-706 1.6L petrol engine, establishing the sole powertrain for the model's entire lifecycle.
keyChanges: Platina LB generation introduced, based on Renault Logan platform
K4M-706 engine fitted to all 1.6i Base and early Comfort trims
Siemens SIRIUS32 ECU management system implemented
knownFor: Simple, robust engine design inherited from Renault
Early injector wear reports emerging in high-mileage examples
Grey import to UK begins, creating niche replacement demand
Compatibility with Renault Logan parts simplifies sourcing

Enhanced oxygen sensor calibration for emissions compliance
Luxury trim added to UK grey import market
knownFor: Running rich symptoms documented at 100,000+ km
Rough idle and stalling linked to injector valve wear
UK specialist knowledge of Renault-platform compatibility grows
Injector replacement cost documented at £515 for cylinder 3

faq.h2: Nissan Platina Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What engine does the Nissan Platina have?
items.answer: The Nissan Platina uses the Renault K4M 1.6L 16-valve petrol engine exclusively across all 2002-2010 models. This Renault-sourced unit produces 109hp and is shared with the Logan, Sandero and Duster platforms.
keyPoints: Sole engine: Renault K4M 1.6L 16V petrol, 108-109hp
Engine codes: K4M-706, K4M-714, K4M-744 depending on year
Compatible with Renault Logan donor engines for UK sourcing
Siemens SIRIUS32 ECU management system
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Platina K4M engine replacement

items.question: Is the Nissan Platina engine the same as the Renault Logan?
items.answer: Yes, the Nissan Platina shares the Renault K4M 1.6L engine with the Logan, Sandero and Duster. This compatibility expands UK parts availability beyond Mexican-import specific suppliers.
keyPoints: Identical K4M engine family across Platina and Logan platforms
Same 1.6L displacement, 16-valve head, 109hp output
Fuel injectors, sensors and ECU are interchangeable
UK Renault breakers can supply compatible K4M units
items.cta: Compare Renault K4M engine prices for your Nissan Platina

items.question: What causes the Nissan Platina engine to run rich?
items.answer: Running rich in the Nissan Platina is primarily caused by faulty fuel injectors (especially cylinder 3) combined with degraded oxygen sensors, leading to over-fuelling, power loss and high consumption.
keyPoints: Defective injector valve on cylinder 3 is the #1 cause
Oxygen sensor failure disrupts air-fuel ratio feedback
Clogged air filters exacerbate rich-running symptoms
Documented at 100,000+ km on 2006 Platina examples
items.cta: Find Nissan Platina running rich repair specialists

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Nissan Platina fuel injector?
items.answer: Replacing a single fuel injector on the Nissan Platina costs £420-£680 from UK specialists, with the documented cylinder 3 repair at £515. A full injector set replacement ranges £480-£780.
keyPoints: Single injector (cylinder 3): £420-£680 specialist price
Full injector set: £480-£780 including ECU adaptation
Dealer pricing typically £650-£1,100 for the same work
Rebuilt K4M engine (£850-£1,700) may offer better value for high mileage
items.cta: Compare Nissan Platina injector replacement vs rebuilt engine costs

items.question: Can I fit a Renault Logan K4M engine in my Nissan Platina?
items.answer: Yes, the Renault Logan K4M engine is directly compatible with the Nissan Platina. This expands UK sourcing options to include Renault breakers and specialists familiar with the shared platform.
keyPoints: Identical K4M engine codes: 706, 714, 744 across both models
Mounting points, wiring and ECU are interchangeable
UK Renault specialists can supply and fit compatible units
Verify engine code matches your Platina's original specification
items.cta: Find UK suppliers of Renault K4M engines for Nissan Platina

items.question: Why does my Nissan Platina battery keep dying?
items.answer: Undervoltage in the Nissan Platina is typically caused by alternator wear combined with an aged battery, triggering ECU faults, dim lights and intermittent starting failures.
keyPoints: Alternator output below 13.8V at idle indicates wear
Battery replacement cost documented at £290 for 2005 Platina
Undervoltage can cause false fault codes and system failures
Test charging system before pursuing expensive ECU repairs
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Platina electrical diagnostic and battery replacement

items.question: Is the Nissan Platina engine reliable?
items.answer: The Nissan Platina's Renault K4M engine is fundamentally robust but suffers from well-documented fuel injector failures at 85k-110k km. With proactive maintenance, it can exceed 200,000 km reliably.
keyPoints: Core mechanical design is proven and simple to maintain
Fuel injector failure on cylinder 3 is the primary weakness
Regular air filter changes and injector cleaning extend life
Rebuilt K4M units with upgraded injectors offer long-term reliability
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Nissan Platina engine prices with warranty

items.question: How much to replace a Nissan Platina engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Nissan Platina K4M engines cost £850-£1,700 supply-only in the UK, with supply & fit adding £450-£850. Used units range £450-£1,300 but require careful inspection.
keyPoints: Rebuilt K4M: £850-£1,700 supply-only, 12-month warranty
Supply & fit labour: £450-£850 depending on location
Used K4M: £450-£1,300, verify compression and service history
Grey import premium applies; Renault donor compatibility reduces cost
items.cta: Get free Nissan Platina engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: What is the engine code for my Nissan Platina?
items.answer: Nissan Platina engine codes are K4M-706 (2002-2005), K4M-714 (2004-2008) or K4M-744 (2006-2010). Check your VIN plate or engine block stamp for the exact code.
keyPoints: K4M-706: Early Base/Comfort models, 2002-2005
K4M-714: Mid-production Comfort/Luxury, 2004-2008
K4M-744: Late Luxury/final imports, 2006-2010
Code stamped on engine block near cylinder head
items.cta: Identify your Nissan Platina engine code and get compatible quotes

items.question: Where can I buy a Nissan Platina engine in the UK?
items.answer: Nissan Platina engines are available through EngineMarket's network of UK specialists familiar with grey-import Renault-platform vehicles, plus Renault Logan breakers offering compatible K4M units.
keyPoints: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK K4M specialists
Renault Logan/Sandero breakers supply compatible donor engines
Mexico-import specialists understand Platina-specific requirements
